summary: Contact Stats Bug Fix VERIFIED. The 'Campaigns' and 'Referrals' counters in the contact card stats bar now correctly display their values. Backend fix in /app/backend/routers/contact_events.py: 1) Campaign count query no longer filters by user_id, so enrollments with salesman_id (from campaign_lifecycle.py) are now counted. 2) Dynamic referral_count added by querying contacts where referred_by matches contact_id. Frontend fix in /app/frontend/app/contact/[id].tsx: Added referral_count to ContactStats interface and displays stats.referral_count ?? contact.referral_count.
critical_code_review_comments: VERIFIED: contact_events.py line 389-391 - Campaign query now only filters by contact_id, NOT user_id - fixes salesman_id enrollment counting
VERIFIED: contact_events.py lines 419-425 - Added dynamic referral_count calculation by querying contacts with referred_by matching contact_id
VERIFIED: contact_events.py line 451 - referral_count included in stats response
VERIFIED: Frontend [id].tsx line 74 - ContactStats interface includes referral_count: number
VERIFIED: Frontend [id].tsx line 371 - Default stats state includes referral_count: 0
VERIFIED: Frontend [id].tsx line 2450 - Stats bar displays stats.referral_count ?? contact.referral_count for 'refs' counter

success_rate.frontend: 100% - Stats bar correctly displays cmpn and refs counters

seed_data_creation: Created test contacts with referred_by relationships and campaign enrollments with both user_id and salesman_id patterns to verify fix handles both enrollment field naming conventions
context_for_next_testing_agent: Contact stats bug fix verified. Test contact: 69a0c06f7626f14d125f8c34 (Forest Ward) - shows stats with 162 tchs, 59 msgs, 4 clks, 1 cmpn, 0 refs. The fix ensures: 1) Campaign count includes enrollments with either user_id or salesman_id fields, 2) Referral count is dynamically calculated from contacts.referred_by, not stale contact.referral_count field.

bug_fix_verification.issue: Contact card stats bar showed 0 for 'Campaigns' and 'Referrals' counters even when contact had campaign enrollments and referrals
root_causes: 1. Campaign enrollments from campaign_lifecycle.py use 'salesman_id' field, but stats query filtered by 'user_id' - so those enrollments weren't counted
2. Referrals displayed stale contact.referral_count field instead of dynamically counting contacts with referred_by matching
